Entra ID::AADSTS293004: The computer name entered in mstsc doesn't match with any one of the "hostnames" attributes for target AADJ device. For example, AADJ device host name is the short name like device_1, but the computer name entered in mstsc is the FQDN like device_1.contoso.com. Try these solutions:
Exchange::4.6.1: ## A migrated function app isn't starting or has some runtime issues - For .NET apps, remember to update the code to .NET 6 before building and deploying. - Double-check the runtime version of the app in the Azure portal. - For Windows apps, you must also set netFrameworkVersion by using CLI or PowerShell. - Make sure you're using the 2.x or later extension bundles, as recommended. Version 4.x of the Functions runtime requires versions 2.x or 3.0.0. - Logging in to Azure Storage using AzureWebJo
Intune::0x80000007: Additional information for error resolution: Use the installation logs and Configuration Manager application logs to determine why installation stopped. Merge the logs so you can easily review what happened before the 0x80000007L error. Use eventvwr.msc to review the Windows event logs for additional events that occurred around the time of the installation failure.
Intune::0x80072f9a: If the Trusted Platform Module (TPM) was reset to factory settings, then the device must reenroll to resume syncing. The device's Microsoft Entra identity is stored in the TPM. So, if the ID is removed, then reenrollment is the only way to reestablish the Microsoft Entra identity. ## Related articles
Intune::0x87D20417: Workaround: Remove all the files from the temp directory specified in the PatchDownloader.log and rerun the ADR. Resolution: Install KB 4600089, Update Rollup for Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager current branch, version 2010.

Microsoft Graph::404: Recent call: If the call was made within the last 150 minutes, the call record might not be generated yet. Wait 150 minutes after the call ended and try again. Old call: If the call is older than 30 days, the call records API returns a 404 Not Found error by design. * Other issues: If neither of the reasons apply, check for any outages reported by the call records API team in the Health tab of the Teams Admin portal. Also, you can open a support ticket with the call records API team for assistan

Microsoft Graph::InvalidRestorePoint: Restore Point ID is invalid or empty.

Microsoft Graph::PcAgentHealthCheckError: ### cloudPcAgentHealthCheckResultType values |Member|Description| |:---|:---| |succeeded|Default. Indicates that the agent passed this health check successfully.| |warning|Indicates that the agent passed this health check with a warning. This result doesn't currently impact agent functionality but requires attention to prevent potential issues.| |failed|Indicates that the agent failed to pass the health check.| |unknownFutureValue|Evolvable enumeration sentinel value. Don't use.|
Microsoft Graph::PcDeviceImageError: ### cloudPcDeviceImageOsStatus values |Member|Description| |:---|:---| |supported|Indicates the Cloud PC device image operating system is active and ready to be used for provisioning.| |supportedWithWarning|Indicates that the Cloud PC device image operating system is expired, but Cloud PC continues to support. If users continue to use it, they may not be able to get security updates.| |unknown|Default. Indicates that the status of the operating system image for the Cloud PC device is unknown.| |

Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_AllOf: The app manifest file failed the allOf validation of the schema.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_AnyOf: The app manifest file failed the anyOf validation of the schema.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Const: The app manifest file failed the const validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the value that failed and {1} represents the correct value.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Contains: The app manifest file failed the contains validation of the schema.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Dependencie: The app manifest file failed the dependencies validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the property that failed.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Else: The app manifest file failed the else validation of the schema.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Enum: The app manifest file failed the enum validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the value that isn't in the enum.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Format: The app manifest file failed the format validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the string that isn't in the expected format and {1} represents the expected format.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Id: The app manifest file failed the id validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the id of the schema.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Maximum: The app manifest file failed the maximum validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the value that exceeds the maximum limit and {1} represents the maximum limit.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_MaximumItem: The app manifest file failed the maximumItems validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the number of elements in the array and {1} represents the maximum allowed.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_MaximumLeng: The app manifest file failed the maximumLength validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the string that exceeds the maximum length and {1} represents the maximum length.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Minimum: The app manifest file failed the minimum validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the value that exceeds the minimum limit and {1} represents the minimum limit.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_MinimumItem: The app manifest file failed the minimumItems validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the number of elements in the array and {1} represents the minimum allowed.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_MinimumLeng: The app manifest file failed the minimumLength validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the string that is less than the minimum length and {1} represents the minimum.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_MultipleOf: The app manifest file failed the multipleOf validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the integer value and {1} indicates the required multiple.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Not: The app manifest file failed the not validation of the schema.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_OneOf: The app manifest file failed the oneOf validation of the schema.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Other: The app manifest file failed other unhandled validations of the schema.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Pattern: The app manifest file failed the pattern validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the value that doesn't match the pattern and {1} represents the expected pattern.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Required: The app manifest file failed the required validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the required properties.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Then: The app manifest file failed the then validation of the schema.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Type: The app manifest file failed the type validation of the schema. Here, {0} represents the string that isn't in the expected type and {1} represents the expected type.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_UniqueItems: The app manifest file failed the uniqueItems validation of the schema.
Microsoft Graph::SchemaError_Valid: The app manifest file failed the valid validation of the schema.
Microsoft Graph::Serialization_FileMissi: The expected file is missing in the app package.
Microsoft Graph::UnknownError: Unknown error occurred. Please try again. If the issue persists, contact support.
Microsoft Graph::ValidDomainsContainsUrl: The domains specified in the localization file must be valid.
Microsoft Graph::VersionCannotBeEmpty: The app package version is missing.
Microsoft Graph::VersionHasMajorLessThan: The app version is less than 1.0. You need to upgrade your app version. For example, if the current version of your app in the store is 1.0 and you’re submitting an update for validation, the app version must be higher than 1.0.
